title: Dark-Zy Canadian Flour Poutine
description: A peppery, keto-friendly Canadian dish featuring strong white flour, pickle, and midnight-craving cravings. Poutine lovers, rejoice!
instructions: Mix 1 tablespoon of black pepper into the flour to give it a peppery kick.
Cut 1 large pickle into small cubes and set aside.
In a large bowl, whisk together the flour and pepper mixture, then slowly add in 1 cup of warm water to form a shaggy dough.
Knead the dough for 5 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Divide the dough into 4 equal pieces and shape each into a ball.
Roll each ball out into a thin sheet, about 1/16 inch thick.
Cut the sheets into long, thin strips, about 1 inch wide.
Fry the strips in a deep skillet with 2 tablespoons of oil over medium heat until they are golden brown and crispy.
Drain the strips on paper towels and set aside.
In a separate pan, heat 1 tablespoon of oil over medium heat and add the pickle cubes.
Cook the pickles for 2-3 minutes until they start to soften, then add in 1 tablespoon of chopped scallions and cook for another minute.

Assemble the poutine by placing a portion of the fried flour strips on the bottom of a bowl, then topping with a spoonful of the pickle mixture, and finally finishing with a sprinkle of grated cheddar cheese.
Bake the poutine in the preheated oven for 10-12 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and bubbly.
